problem_statement: A university room-scheduling service accepts calendar feeds from several departments, yet some recurring reservations stopped appearing in room displays after a routine build refresh. The feed fetches are successful and unrelated reservations continue to update.

observations.statement: The first missing reservation was processed by build 6.14.2 at 2026-06-22T09:12:41Z; build 6.14.1 had processed the same department's prior feed without omissions.
observations.provenance: retained ingest ledger and build deployment record

observations.statement: Affected calendar components contain a folded TZID property whose continuation line begins with one tab; unaffected components either have no TZID or keep the property on one line.
HTTP records show complete 200 responses with matching byte counts and hashes at the fetcher and archive boundary, ruling against truncation in transit.
observations.provenance: gateway access logs and feed archive index

observations.statement: The application source revision and parser invocation settings are byte-identical between builds 6.14.1 and 6.14.2, while the resolved calendar parsing package changed from 4.8.3 to 4.9.0 through a transitive lock refresh.
observations.provenance: signed build manifests and dependency lock diff

observations.statement: Parser diagnostics in the newer build mark the continuation as an orphan property and drop the enclosing event before recurrence expansion.

An offline parse of the retained feed with the two locked package versions produces 48 events with 4.8.3 and 41 events with 4.9.0; the seven-event difference exactly matches the room-display omissions.
observations.provenance: isolated read-only replay report using archived packages

observations.statement: A retained negative-control feed with equivalent recurrence rules but no folded properties produces identical event sets under both package versions.
observations.provenance: isolated replay report for control fixture

observations.statement: Database and display-service records agree on the reduced set of 41 events, placing the first divergence before persistence rather than in display filtering or cache refresh.
observations.provenance: database audit export and display cache inventory

observations.statement: No timezone database, host locale, department configuration, or feed-authentication change appears in the signed build and environment manifests for the incident interval.
